column1: Data Supplied By: National Livestock Reporting Service
Data source: National Livestock Reporting Service
Please see terms of use here: https://www.mla.com.au/general/Terms-and-conditions/data-and-information/

Light rain overnight and intermittent showers throughout the sale saw Singleton agents yard 1252 cattle, 300 head less than last week. Supplementary fed trade cattle making up to 568c/kg the highlight.

Fair supplies of light steer calves sold to slightly dearer rates making from 366c to 496c/kg, medium weights cheaper by 5c to 12c/kg and sold from 406c to 490c/kg. Light heifer calves saw a rise of 25c/kg trading from 310c to 388c/kg, B muscles out to 420c/kg and light trade calves to 430c/kg. Heavy trade calves around 400kg sold between 466c and 568c/kg. 

Heavy yearling steers over 400kg to the lot feeders 14c/kg dearer making from 430c to 468c/kg and same weight heifers traded between 380c and 424c/kg averaging 418c/kg. Heavy grown heifers and Bullocks to process 350c to 430c/kg.

Around 200 cows offered lighter types to process either side of firm 205c to 310c/kg, heavy cows with weight, shape and condition 9c to 24c/kg better off making between 302c and 368c/kg.

Market Operator Angus Barlow
